mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC > -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals extreme labor market tightness that forces the Federal Reserve to pause rate hikes, triggering a short-term equity rally over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances when UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflation that compels aggressive monetary tightening, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative 60-day returns (logistic regression coefficient not significant at p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the index of industrial production following a contraction phase signals supply-side recovery that improves corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases do not precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (mean forward return <= 0%).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 2.5% trigger immediate equity deleveraging as borrowing costs exceed historical averages, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day S&P 500 direction (Rank IC between -0.02 and 0.02 over 5-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside contracting real GDP signal a synchronized demand collapse that reduces equity risk appetite, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in HOUST and GDPC1 fail to predict negative 20-day returns (probability of negative return < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0% quarter-over-quarter during supply-constrained periods resolves bottleneck fears, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 103 do not precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns (mean forward return <= 0% over 3-year window).
